_label: Sun & Rain Works' conceptual claim to art at auction (2009)

identified_by.content: Sun & Rain Works' conceptual claim to art at auction (2009)

content: Sun & Rain Works asserts a conceptual claim: that in 2009, in connection with the Sotheby's Photographs auction (sale N08533, New York), it designated its auction engagement — the consignment of an Ansel Adams photograph, 'San Francisco from Twin Peaks' (lot 16), together with the associated website www.wtfisart.com — as Metaconceptual Art, framed as the work The Auction as Artwork. This is the artist's own designation, made explicit over time. It is not a publicly witnessed live-creation event and has not been adjudicated as a historical fact; the Sotheby's catalogue lists the lot as an Ansel Adams photograph and does not name Sun & Rain Works. This statement makes no claim of being 'first', and does not name, compare against, or dispute any later artist or event; any such comparison would require independent recognition and separately published evidence.

about._label: Sun & Rain Works' auction engagement (Sotheby's Photographs, 2009)

referred_to_by.content: Supporting documentation is described at evidence-live-auction-2009 and includes 2008 Sotheby's correspondence and the public catalogue lot. Recommended framing: the artist's asserted conceptual designation of an auction engagement. Avoided claims: 'first in history', publicly witnessed live creation, or precedence over any later artist or event, absent independent recognition and separately published evidence.
